member.name: Ayoub Khan
member.synopsis: Ayoub Khan is the Independent MP for Birmingham Perry Barr , and has been an MP continually since 4 July 2024.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Birmingham
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what steps his Department is taking to support Birmingham City Council in managing the potential financial implications of the cancellation of the Birmingham Highways PFI contrac
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of the statutory intervention that commenced in October 2023, Commissioners continue to support Birmingham City Council’s improvement and recovery. My department regularly engages with both the Council and Commissioners, in line with standard prac...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Birmingham
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what discussions his Department has had with the Birmingham City Council Commissioners on the potential financial impact of the cancellation of the Birmingham Highways PFI contra
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of the statutory intervention that commenced in October 2023, Commissioners continue to support Birmingham City Council’s improvement and recovery. My department regularly engages with both the Council and Commissioners, in line with standard prac...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Birmingham
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment she has made of the potential impact of the cancellation of the Birmingham Highways PFI contract on Birmingham City Council’s capital spending requirements.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to working with the Council to achieve a successful transition away from its PFI arrangements alongside progress with the Council’s financial management and recovery. To support this, the Department for Transport has put in pla...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Birmingham
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether she plans to publish her Department’s most recent decision letter regarding the Final Business Case for the Birmingham Highways PFI contract.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The letter from DfT officials will be deposited in the House of Commons Library.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy and Transport: Finance
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, whether her Department has conducted an assessment of the potential economic impact from (i) cancellation, (ii) delay, (iii) descoping, (iv) or reprofiling of energy and transport projects to fund increases in defen
writtenQuestions.answerText: A safer, more secure country is the right environment for the investment needed to deliver economic growth. This Government’s commitment to the fiscal rules is non-negotiable. To fund the Defence Investment Plan within the fiscal rules, departments have b...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Treasury

writtenQuestions.heading: Roads: Birmingham
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether her Department has made an assessment of the potential impact of the cancellation of the Birmingham Highways PFI contract on Birmingham City Council’s financial sustainability.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to working with the Council to achieve a successful transition away from its PFI arrangements alongside progress on improving the Council’s financial sustainability. To support this, the Department for Transport has put in plac...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what assessment his Department has made of the impact of extreme heat on outdoor workers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Hon. member to the response to UIN 14088.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Conservation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what assessment his Department has made of the effectiveness of Government-funded home energy efficiency and retrofit schemes in reducing the risk of overheating during periods of extreme hea
writtenQuestions.answerText: As set out in the Warm Homes Plan, our buildings are not currently built to deal with record summer temperatures and some are at significant risk of overheating. UK Health Security Agency (UKHSA) has estimated there have been 10,000 excess deaths caused b...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, how many instances of heat-related speed restrictions on the rail network occurred in each of the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Network Rail has recorded the following number of daily incidents of “Critical Rail Temperature speed-restriction incidents” as follows: Financial YearHeat speeds (Incident-days)2019/205272020/215752021/2210802022/2316222023/246242024/253322025/268032026/...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Energy: Conservation
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what steps his Department is taking to ensure that future home retrofit programmes incorporate measures to improve low-income households' resilience to extreme heat.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Warm Homes Plan sets out our approach to upgrading the nation’s homes, including climate adaptation. Over this Parliament, we intend to introduce the most appropriate, cost-effective passive cooling measures into our capital schemes, to support low-in...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Energy Security and Net Zero, what estimate his Department has made of the number of households unable to afford cooling appliances during periods of extreme heat.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department does not hold this information. The Warm Homes Plan sets out our approach to upgrading the nation’s homes, including climate adaptation. Over this Parliament, we intend to introduce the most appropriate, cost-effective passive cooling measu...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Energy Security and Net Zero

writtenQuestions.heading: Railways: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the resilience of the rail network to increasing summer temperatures.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department recognises that increasing summer temperatures can affect the safe and reliable operation of the railway, including through impacts on track, signalling and power infrastructure. The rail industry has established procedures to manage these ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Public Transport: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what steps her Department is taking to improve passenger safety and comfort on public transport during periods of extreme heat.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ensuring the welfare of passengers on public transport remains a priority for the Department, including during extreme hot weather. Train operators, along with other public transport operators, encourage all passengers to follow the latest public health a...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Transport

writtenQuestions.heading: Housing: Construction
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, whether he plans to update building regulations to require new homes to be designed to reduce overheating during periods of extreme heat.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Building Regulations Part O, introduced in 2021 and in force since June 2022, already requires new residential buildings to be designed to mitigate the risk of overheating, including by reducing unwanted solar heat gains and enabling the removal of excess...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Cremation and Funerals
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the adequacy of consumer protection provided by at-need funeral, cremation and burial services offered by Local Authorities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government does not collect this information and has therefore not done any assessments. The Department of Health and Social Care is leading the cross-government response to the Fuller Inquiry and will publish a full response to the Inquiry’s recommen...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Cremation and Funerals: Local Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what assessment he has made of the value for money of at-need funeral, cremation and burial services offered by Local Authorities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government does not collect this information and has therefore not done any assessments. The Department of Health and Social Care is leading the cross-government response to the Fuller Inquiry and will publish a full response to the Inquiry’s recommen...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Cremation and Funerals: Local Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what information his Department collects annually from Local Authorities on the provision of cremation and burial services; and if he will publish that data.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government does not collect this information and has therefore not done any assessments. The Department of Health and Social Care is leading the cross-government response to the Fuller Inquiry and will publish a full response to the Inquiry’s recommen...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Funerals: Local Government
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, what information his Department collects annually from Local Authorities on the provision of pre-need and at-need funeral services; and if he will publish that data.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government does not collect this information and has therefore not done any assessments. The Department of Health and Social Care is leading the cross-government response to the Fuller Inquiry and will publish a full response to the Inquiry’s recommen...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Employment: Temperature
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, what guidance his Department provides to employers on protecting workers from extreme heat.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the Right Honourable member to the response to UIN 13128.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Work and Pensions

writtenQuestions.heading: Funerals: Advertising
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what discussions he has had with local authorities on their compliance with advertising standards for the provision of funeral and cremation services.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Ministry of Justice is responsible for the legislative framework to which local authorities’ provision of burial and cremation is subject. However, we do not have day-to-day operational oversight of public cemeteries and crematoria, which are managed ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Justice
